text.title: flexibility-efficiency Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for flexibility-efficiency as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Expert-only interface — all options always visible, no progressive disclosure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Overwhelming novice users causes abandonment before they reach the point of becoming regular users; progressive disclosure is not optional for complex tools.

failure-mode.description: Novice-only interface — no accelerators, every action requires full guided flow.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Power users who repeat the same guided flow dozens of times daily experience significant productivity loss and frustration.

failure-mode.description: Over-customization — too many configuration options confuse users.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Customization beyond the 20% of settings users actually change adds cognitive load without benefit; limit customization to high-frequency actions and layout.
